description: Letter in Judaeo-Arabic from Avraham b. ʿAṭā' (=Natan), in Malīj, to Abū Zikrī Yehuda b. Mūsā. The writer is not the same person as the Nagid of Qayrawān with the same name. The letter seems to be a response to Yehuda's request that the writer take good care of a certain Abū Isḥāq (al-qawām bihi). Avraham reports, "Our companions (aṣḥābunā) did not fall short with him even before your letter arrived. When it arrived, I took him into my own house. I will look after him (aqūm bi-ḥālihi) until he recovers. He is doing better than before. Whatever I [have to] spend, he is secure (muthbat) with me until he recovers. [I would do this] even if I did not owe you anything and you asked me to do it." ASE.
tags: Illness, illness: location, illness letter 969-1517, illness: nursing, illness: cost
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מאברהם בן עטא (=נתן), במלי'ג', לאבו זכרי יהודה בן מוסא. אברהם מדווח שדאג לאבו יצחק: 'הכנסתי אותו לביתי. אדאג לו עד שיבריא... גם אם לא הייתי חייב לך כלום.'
